# Theorem agent onboarding

This file is the single source a coding agent can read to get started on
Theorem. It is intentionally operational: choose the right front door, ground in
the repo, and prove changes with the narrowest real oracle.

## First commands

```bash
git pull
sed -n '1,220p' AGENTS.md
sed -n '1,220p' CLAUDE.md
```

The repo root is the working directory. There is no root Cargo workspace.
`rustyredcore_THG/` is the main Cargo workspace; apps under `apps/` are mostly
standalone roots.

## Fast developer install

Use the `theorem` wrapper as the dev front door:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Travis-Gilbert/Theorem/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
theorem init
theorem once "hello from Theorem"
```

`theorem init` writes a no-secret starter `theorem-localmodel.toml`. It uses:

- deterministic `rule` model provider
- local MCP URL `http://127.0.0.1:8380/mcp`
- tenant slug `Travis-Gilbert`
- receiver, capture, and relay disabled
- ledger `.theorem/localmodel-token-ledger.jsonl`

Do not put secrets in TOML, docs, AGENTS.md, or committed files. Hosted sync is
opt-in. If a live model is needed, set `[model].provider = "openai-compatible"`
and point it at a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint; keep API keys in environment
variables.

## Human front door

The desktop app lives at `apps/desktop/`. It is the human-facing local-first
front door. The CLI is for developers and agents; the desktop is for people.

## Common build and test oracles

```bash
cd rustyredcore_THG && cargo test -p theorem-localmodel
cd rustyredcore_THG && cargo test -p theorem-harness-core
cd rustyredcore_THG && cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime
cd apps/theorem-harness-node && npm run build:debug && npm run smoke
```

Pick the oracle for the files you changed. Do not claim product success from
code inspection alone.

## Harness rules

- Tenant slug is `Travis-Gilbert`.
- Use the Harness for grounded work: recall before answering past-decision
  questions, announce coordination footprint when multiple heads may overlap,
  and encode only load-bearing decisions or reusable lessons.
- Coordinate by footprint, not file ownership.
- If another head already has dirty changes, preserve them and keep edits scoped.

## Repository map

- `rustyredcore_THG/crates/theorem-localmodel/`: local assistant daemon and MCP tool
  host; the `theorem` wrapper starts this.
- `rustyredcore_THG/crates/theorem-receiver/`: dispatch receiver that launches
  Claude or Codex sessions from jobs.
- `rustyredcore_THG/crates/theorem-harness-core/`: pure harness kernel.
- `rustyredcore_THG/crates/theorem-harness-runtime/`: GraphStore-backed runtime,
  memory, jobs, and coordination persistence.
- `rustyredcore_THG/crates/rustyred-thg-mcp/`: MCP server surface at `POST /mcp`.
- `apps/theorem-harness-server/`: HTTP transport for runs, rooms, mentions,
  jobs, and push/SSE.
- `apps/desktop/`: Tauri + React desktop shell.
- `docs/site/`: public docs.
- `docs/plans/`: implementation plans and handoffs.

## Editing discipline

Read the named spec or source before editing. If a spec names a path, signature,
or reference output, treat that as a requirement by position. For Rust work,
prefer focused package tests from the relevant workspace. For UI work, preserve
tokens, focus states, reduced motion, and measured accessibility claims.

Commits use `<type>(<scope>): <description>`, with a required scope. Do not add
`Co-Authored-By` lines.
